description_long: This is a small, rectangular wooden cabin with horizontally striped brown siding and a simple, functional design. The roof is a dark gray gable type with a single chimney protruding from one side, suggesting a fireplace or stove inside. The front facade features a small covered porch with steps leading up to a central door, flanked symmetrically by two square windows. The rear side of the cabin has two additional windows, while the shorter sides each have a single window centered beneath the gable. The porch is supported by posts and has a modest overhang, providing shelter at the entrance. The overall appearance is rustic and utilitarian, typical of a log cabin found in rural or suburban neighborhoods.
description: A small, rustic wooden cabin with a gable roof, central porch, and symmetrical window arrangement. The exterior features brown horizontal siding and a prominent chimney.
description_view_0: This view shows the rear of the cabin with two square windows and the full expanse of the gable roof.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the single centered window beneath the gable and the chimney on the roof.
description_view_2: The front view displays the covered porch, central door, steps, and two flanking windows.
description_view_3: This side view mirrors the opposite end, showing a single window beneath the gable and the porch extending from the front.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with moderate overhang and a single chimney, covered in dark material
attribute_window_arrangement: Five square windows: two symmetrically placed on the front, two symmetrically placed on the rear, and one centered on each short side, all of similar size and shape, creating a balanced and regular pattern
attribute_door_placement: A single exterior door is centered on the front facade, accessed via a porch and steps
